review_title: Since I've been using PRTG Enterprise Monitor my work has become much easier, it's a tool that you.
description: I have been using PRTG for over 10 years in my career as a IT Network/Systems Admin. This is good experience.
author: Abdel fattah E.
pros: Good products and services PRTG Enterprise Monitor offers architectural flexibility for large IT environments, which allows you to deploy sensors across an unlimited number of PRTG servers. Use the flexibility to add or remove sensors to meet the needs of your individual IT environment
cons: For any IT team it is absolutely clear that the network infrastructure is the backbone of any...I have been using PRTG for over 10 years in my career as a IT Network/Systems Admin. There are...

review_title: Awesome software but pricey, but worth every penny
description: Limited issues and if I do have an issue there’s already a KB article for it. Easy to troubleshoot, easy to work on, 100% reliable
author: Wil S.
pros: The best part of PRTG is how easy it is to setup new probes and how easy it is to add sensors. The software provides detailed descriptions of what the sensors does.
cons: Cons, sadly it is pricey when you have over 100 sensors. Overall worth the price for the peace of mind it gives.

review_title: Decent product, but not great business practices
description: The product itself is fine, but I ultimately will never recommend PRTG to anyone. I believe in 2024 they changed their billing model. Previously we had purchased a perpetual license and paid an annual maintenance cost for updates and support. this made it very cost effective. PRTG changed to a subscription only model and the price went up annually for us by 5x. I would have considered sticking with PRTG still but they handled the communication on this very poorly. There was a cutoff date for being able to buy the maintenance, which would have allowed us to stay on the legacy model for a while. We were notified about the model change 1 day after the cutoff. It seemed like they intentionally did not notify us until after the cutoff. I tried to work with PRTG to find a resolution but they were unwilling to work with us. Because of this we opted to invest out resources elsewhere.
author: Nick F.
pros: PRTG allows the ability to scan a large amount of probe types in your environment. It is very robust.
cons: PRTG is fairly complex and difficult to get just right. We used PRTG a bit different as we are a IT provider. We used it for monitoring external connections on client locations so we didn't use a lot of the auto-scan functionality. We only cared about up/down for specific devices. It was difficult to use it in this parsed down way at times.

review_title: IT-Systemadministrator
description: We have been using PRTG for almost 10 years now and are extremely satisfied. You have a complete overview of whether all services and systems are functioning properly on a dashboard throughout the day.
author: Andre S.
pros: PRTG is a clear tool that allows you to monitor your entire IT environment, especially your network environment. A wide variety of services can be monitored with different threshold values. These messages can also be integrated into other systems, such as a ticket system.
cons: It takes a little time to get started and to connect and monitor all devices. There are no ready-made sensors for some devices and systems, which is why manual queries have to be created.

review_title: PRTG for AD Replication
description: As we have have distributed AD across multiple sites, we have used PRTG to report on AD synchronization and therefore help us to react quickly if there are replication failures
author: Ross B.
pros: Free edition is a useful way to evaluate and monitor your core infrastructure
cons: Web GUI sometimes slow to respond or update

review_title: Not my favourite monitoring tool but worth a look
description: PRTG does what it says on the tin, and in fairness it does it very well. It's reliable and it's flexible, I find their free SNMP monitoring tool extremely helpful when troubleshooting issues with monitoring older routers/firewalls/printers etc. We didn't have the cloud version but another chap I know who does in IT said the mobile app and push notifications were a life saver. The ability to monitor SLA's as well is also handy for working towards KPI's and keeps the management team happy, coupled with the extremely customisable reports.
author: Rian S.
pros: The best thing by far about PRTG Enterprise was the ability to have multiple PRTG server instances in different locations, but all visible within one 'master' view. This was particularly helpful when you have sites across seas like I did when I used it, in Latvia and Slovenia. They had their own PRTG server that the remote IT hands could monitor and keep on top of, but as Infrastructure Manager it allowed me to also keep an eye on things and help out where needed.
cons: It's hard to describe, but I just didn't like the UI of PRTG in general, the interface didn't feel as nice and user-friendly as other tools and just seemed to be less intuitive and aesthetically pleasing. But that's not to say that it lacks any features and it may well have just been me that couldn't figure out how to make it look 'nice'.

review_title: MY REVIEW PRTG Enterprise Monitor
description: My experience with PRTG Enterprise Monitor has been very good. It makes it easy to monitor networks, servers, and applications in one place, and the reports and alerts help detect issues quickly. The setup takes some time at first, and the licensing can be costly if you need many sensors, but overall it’s a strong and reliable solution for large organizations.
author: mohamed H.
pros: User-friendly interface – provides clear dashboards and visual graphs. Comprehensive monitoring – supports networks, servers, applications, databases, and cloud services. Smart alerts and notifications – via email, SMS, or mobile apps. High scalability – suitable for large enterprises managing thousands of devices. Integration with major platforms – such as Microsoft Azure and Amazon AWS. Powerful reporting – ability to create detailed or custom reports for analysis. High flexibility – supports multiple protocols (SNMP, WMI, HTTP, Ping, Flow
cons: High cost compared to standard versions, especially with a large number of sensors. Requires technical expertise to manage efficiently in large environments. Resource intensive if used with thousands of sensors without proper optimization. Complex customization – setting up templates or integrations can be time-consuming. Web interface performance issues with extremely large datasets.

review_title: PRTG: Powerful Monitoring Made Simple
description: PRTG Enterprise Monitor by Paessler is easy to use, reliable, and provides comprehensive monitoring for servers, networks, and applications. The dashboard and alerts are very helpful for proactive issue management. The downside to PRTG is the sensor-based licensing, which can get costly for larger infrastructures. Overall, it's a solid and efficient monitoring tool, best for small to medium-sized organizations.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: What I like most about PRTG is its ease of use and all-in-one monitoring approach. A customizable dashboard and real-time alerts make it easy to track server, network, and application health in one place. Their customer support is good, fast, and reliable.
cons: 1. Sensor-based licensing - can get expensive for larger infrastructures. 2. Mobile/Web limitations - fewer features compared to the full desktop client. 3. Learning curve - advanced configurations and custom sensors take time to master.

review_title: Highly Customizable and Reliable Monitoring Tool
description: 10/10 – Ideal for medium to large enterprises looking for a reliable and flexible monitoring solution.
author: Pawandip K.
pros: We've been using PRTG Enterprise Monitor and have found it extremely helpful in managing our IT infrastructure. The platform is highly customizable, allowing us to tailor sensors, alerts, and dashboards to match our unique network needs. Its intuitive interface makes it easy to deploy, and the breadth of monitoring options—from bandwidth to application performance—is impressive. The flexibility and scalability of PRTG help us stay ahead of issues and reduce downtime significantly. It's become a vital part of our IT operations.
cons: Initial setup in large environments can be time-consuming due to the need for extensive sensor configuration, custom templates, user role setup, alert tuning, and integration with other systems. While powerful, PRTG requires careful planning and manual effort to fully optimize for enterprise-scale networks.

review_title: Reliable Monitoring Tool with Great Support
description: Overall, my experience has been positive. It gives a clear view of the entire network and makes troubleshooting faster. There are small areas of improvement, like simplifying reports and reducing false alarms, but the reliability and strong support team make it worth using.
author: Basant Kumar D.
pros: Customer support is too good and and responsive. Dashboard is easy to understand and real time monitoring helps alot
cons: Sometimes the interface feels a bit cluttered when too many sensors are added. The initial setup can be slightly complex for beginners, and occasional false alerts can be frustrating.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: PRTG Enterprise Monitor : la supervision réseau à la fois simple, visuelle et puissante
description: Facilité d'installation : L’installation de base est relativement rapide et bien documentée. Le logiciel détecte automatiquement une grande partie de l’infrastructure via l’auto-discovery. Découverte réseau efficace : Pratique pour les environnements déjà en production. Il est possible de découvrir des dizaines ou centaines d’équipements très rapidement. Courbe d’apprentissage modérée : Facile à utiliser au début, mais les utilisateurs avancés devront investir du temps pour maîtriser les capteurs personnalisés, les modèles, les dépendances, etc. Expérience vécue : On peut rapidement commencer à monitorer les équipements critiques (switches, serveurs, firewalls) en quelques heures seulement après l'installation.
author: HERIMALALA RANAIVOAR R.
pros: Interface utilisateur intuitive L'interface web est claire et bien conçue, facilitant la prise en main même pour les utilisateurs moins expérimentés. La navigation entre les capteurs, les cartes réseau (maps) et les rapports est fluide. Visualisation des données puissante (Maps & Dashboards) Les cartes personnalisables (PRTG Maps) permettent de créer des vues dynamiques du réseau avec des éléments visuels en temps réel. Très utile pour les salles de supervision (NOC). Grande couverture de capteurs intégrés Support natif de nombreux protocoles (SNMP, WMI, NetFlow, sFlow, Ping, HTTP, etc.). Beaucoup de capteurs prêts à l’emploi, y compris pour les environnements cloud, VMware, Microsoft 365, etc. Évolutivité avec l'édition Enterprise Permet la gestion centralisée de plusieurs instances de PRTG via ITOps Board. Adapté aux grandes infrastructures réparties sur plusieurs sites ou réseaux. Alerting puissant et personnalisable Système d’alertes conditionnelles (multi-états) avec notifications par e-mail, SMS, push mobile, etc. Possibilité de créer des seuils d’alerte très fins selon les besoins. Modèle de licence clair Paiement basé sur le nombre de capteurs, sans coûts cachés pour les modules supplémentaires. Support technique réactif L’assistance de Paessler est généralement bien notée pour sa réactivité et sa pertinence.
cons: Montée en charge (scalabilité) limitée sans Enterprise La version standard peut devenir moins performante au-delà de quelques milliers de capteurs sur une seule instance. D’où l'intérêt de l'édition Enterprise pour les environnements très étendus. Déploiement initial long sur de très gros environnements Nécessite une phase de configuration assez importante si l'on souhaite un monitoring très personnalisé. Le paramétrage avancé des capteurs peut être fastidieux. Personnalisation des rapports limitée Bien que des rapports soient disponibles, ils manquent parfois de souplesse ou de profondeur (notamment pour certains besoins spécifiques de reporting). Consommation de ressources PRTG peut être gourmand en ressources serveur, surtout avec de nombreux capteurs à intervalle de sondage court. Documentation parfois trop généraliste Certaines sections de la documentation manquent de cas d’usage concrets ou avancés. Toutefois, la communauté est active et fournit souvent des solutions.

review_title: PRTG Enterprise Monitor: Powerful, Scalable, and Centralized IT Infrastructure Monitoring
description: RTG Enterprise Monitor provides end-to-end monitoring across large, distributed IT infrastructures. Users appreciate the ability to track networks, applications, virtual environments, cloud systems, and IoT devices—all from a centralized platform. This makes it particularly valuable for enterprises with complex, multi-site operations
author: Prem Ananth K.
pros: PRTG Enterprise Monitor enables monitoring of each location separately, each with its own dashboard, or monitoring all locations with one central overview. This flexibility allows for comprehensive visibility into the entire network infrastructure
cons: Implementing PRTG Enterprise Monitor in extensive IT setups can be intricate. Users have noted that the deployment process is fairly complex, especially in large environments, requiring careful planning and expertise.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Great Product for Monitoring
description: We use it for monitoring of our accounting platform. It monitors required services for several of our operations.
author: Scott B.
pros: This is a great product for monitoring of hardware and services and up time for servers and all required services
cons: Adjusting of some of the email alerts is tricky, some of the emails do give the end user the ability to pause alerts though

review_title: the ideal monitoring tool for large IT infrastructures
description: For any IT team it is absolutely clear that the network infrastructure is the backbone of any current company regardless of its size, and based on this, investing in tools that allow monitoring from the macro to the micro all the details of operation, processes and others makes the company feel confident in its deployment of network equipment and the operation of this, PRTG Enterprise Monitor provides all these features with the added bonus of a technical support team that we have received the collaboration of when required.
author: Carolina R.
pros: Our company is distributed in several LATAM countries and all its IT infrastructure is located in various datacenters located in some of its major cities, this topology has required a tool with which to have a global vision but in turn detailed of the different data centers and each of the teams that make it up, in turn, services, protocols and other additional elements that are required for proper operation; the control panel is extremely simplified but at the same time powerful and allows with a few clicks to add new data centers as the network expands and immediately we have the visualization of the elements that are required, this is simplicity and effectiveness of the tool.
cons: It is clear that PRTG Enterprise Monitor is focused on huge IT infrastructures which have many elements and processes that need to be monitored, this translates notoriously in operating costs since it must be taken into account that the license for these cases depends on the number of sensors required, although looking at it from a preventive point of view will be reflected in the initial ROI as it will allow correcting failures before they cause considerable operating expenses.

review_title: Covers all the bases for mid-sized corporate infrastructure
description: We are using PRTG to monitor all aspects of our network and server infrastructure. After trialling a few products, PRTG was selected several years ago for the extensive feature set at a reasonable cost of ownership. So far we are completely satisfied with the product. PRTG is right-sized for us without all the features we need. We have integrated PRTG with Grafana to produce extremely user-friendly dashboards for our management and the integration was a breeze.
author: Ranga S.
pros: Ease of use Balanced cost to features Detailed dashboards and graphs Realtime Alerts/notifications Continuous updates and new sensors/features added regularly Easy integration with dashboard software (ie. Grafana)
cons: Heavy use of resources (I do not believe this is a product issue as we are sharing our hardware resource for other admin utilities)

review_title: Motoring world
author: peter O.
pros: The system is easy to setup; using the auto discovery that help to bring up the most relevant sensors to the device being monitored. The network diagrams gave us great insight when troubleshooting issue and pinpoint where the root is. It allows us to be always pro active and provide the uptime required by the business without manually carry out the tasks.
cons: The default parameter could provide some false positive alerts; some adjustment is needed to make alerts more suitable to the company

review_title: PRTG is best we can have
description: Just loved it.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Loved the flexibility provision for monitoring, exact detailed. Thanks
cons: As far I'm using still haven't observed any cons still.

review_title: Monitoring
description: Overall experience has been good, it does what it says and it has been very useful for monitoring our systems.
author: Matt K.
pros: Many sensors, easy to setup compared to competitors. Very customizable in alerting and what you can monitor.
cons: Doesn't scale well once to 10k+ sensors, vendor recommendation against virtualization. Price increasing and licensing scheme.

review_title: Excellent Software with reasonable price
description: Good
author: Ong J.
pros: Ease of use, easy to setup and utilise minimum server resource
cons: Network map can be improved as well as reporting in terms of network quality

review_title: Very Useful
description: We can see when sites are down and are able to monitor the situation.
author: Charles H.
pros: That we can setup a wall to see everything.
cons: Customization is a little tricky in the beginning

review_title: Affordable and Customizable
description: I have been using PRTG for years, and it has always been reliable and easy to use.
author: Bethany C.
pros: This software is very reasonably priced, and you can customize the sensors to match the needs of your environment.
cons: It does take a while to set everything up if you make everything custom, but it is worth it.

review_title: Paolo Islamaj review
author: Paolo I.
pros: very user friendly and responsive in real time to all alerts
cons: nothing about the software for me is working fine without any change needed

review_title: Great product - I use it daily
description: Excellent experience from installation through deployment and growth
author: Bill R.
pros: The ease of maintenance, reliability of alerts and ease of monitoring without agents.
cons: A bit complicated to configure anything except basic alerts. Not much integration with third parties.

review_title: PRTG is my single-pane of glass
description: Great :)
author: Marcel K.
pros: Easy integration and fast setup. Love the preconfigured sensors
cons: Clean up the startpage, there are too much graphs that doesn't help

review_title: Easy tu use
description: From installing to getting your first things monitored is short.
author: Christian R.
pros: Easy to setup and use. The price tag is attractive.
cons: The GUI can feel a bit old and needs a newer look.
